data.title: Short bets against S&P 500 healthcare stock rises in March; MRNA stays top shorted
data.description:
In March, short sellers collectively increased their negative bets on the broader healthcare industry, as indicated by a rise in the average short interest for the S&P 500 Health Care Index Sector (NYSEARCA:XLV) to 1.81%, up from 1.79% in February. Within the sector, Moderna maintained its position as the stock with the highest level of short interest for the month.

The healthcare sector, as represented by the Health Care Select Sector SPDR® Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLV) which holds a 12.12% weight in the S&P 500, has experienced a relatively smaller decline of 0.93% since the start of the year. This contrasts with the broader S&P 500 index (SP500), which has seen a more significant decrease of 10.30% during the same period.

 

Stocks with the largest and least short positions

Ranked by short interest as a percentage of shares float

Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA)—most shorted—saw short interest rose to 14.58% from 13.19% in February. Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Ratings, the biotechnology company focused on developing messenger RNA ((mRNA)) therapeutics and vaccines has a Strong Sell rating with a score of 1.19 out of 5.

Seeking Alpha analysts Stephen Ayers and Terry Chrisomalis discussed the potential impact of management changes at a regulatory agency on vaccine makers. Ayers believes the Trump administration's stance on vaccines could create market uncertainty, as seen in Moderna's (NASDAQ:MRNA) stock drop. Ayers also notes a potential "snowball effect" for mRNA companies due to existing public wariness, which could be worsened by increased regulatory scrutiny, impacting the entire vaccine lifecycle from development to market adoption. Increased regulatory scrutiny could affect vaccine developers like Moderna, Pfizer, and BioNTech, leading to stricter data requirements, extended development timelines, and increased application rejection.

ResMed (NYSE:RMD) came in second with a short interest of 6.10%, followed by DaVita (NYSE:DVA) with a short interest of 5.82%.

Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) was the least shorted stock in March, with a short interest of 0.79%, followed by Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) which came in a close second with a short percentage of 0.83%. Medtronic (NYSE:MDT) was the third least shorted company in March, with a short percentage of 0.85%.

 

Industry Analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

 

For the twelfth consecutive month in March, biotechnology held the position of the most shorted industry within the healthcare index. Short interest in this sub-sector saw a slight increase to 2.63%, up from 2.62% in February. The healthcare provider and services sector followed with a short interest of 1.88% at the end of March. In contrast, the pharmaceuticals industry recorded the lowest short interest among the healthcare sub-sectors in March, with a short percentage of 1.60%.

Related ETFs: Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLV), SPDR S&P Pharmaceuticals ETF (NYSEARCA:XPH), VanEck Pharmaceutical ETF (NASDAQ:PPH), iShares Nasdaq Biotechnology ETF (NASDAQ:IBB), iShares U.S. Pharmaceuticals ETF (NYSEARCA:IHE), and Invesco Nasdaq Biotechnology ETF (NASDAQ:IBBQ).

data.title: Better Choice Company reinstates stock buyback program, increases authorization to 6.5M
data.description:
• Better Choice (NYSE:BTTR) has reinstated its stock repurchase program and increased authorization up to $6.5M of its outstanding shares of common stock, until December 31, 2025.
  • The company repurchased 102,405 common shares at an average price of $1.9869 per share under the previous $5M stock repurchase program, which expired on December 31, 2024.
  • BTTR -1.6% premarket to $1.84.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BP Chair Lund re-elected with significant protest vote
data.description:
BP (NYSE:BP) CEO Murray Auchincloss and Chair Helge Lund were re-elected at Thursday's annual general meeting, Reuters reported, although the lower level of shareholder support for Lund could mean his previously announced departure occurs sooner than the expected 2026 timetable.

BP's (NYSE:BP) board was up for re-election as the company faces activist shareholder Elliott Management and criticism from climate-focused investors that wanted a protest vote against Lund.

Auchincloss received 97% of votes and Lund an unusually low 75.7%, according to provisional results; board members need 50% of votes to be elected and typically achieve tallies near 100%.

Separately, BP (BP) said it has loaded the first cargo of liquefied natural gas for export from its Greater Tortue Ahmeyim Phase 1 project offshore Mauritania and Senegal.

The project has been hampered by technical, financial, and Covid-related issues, and originally was expected to begin LNG exports in 2022.

GTA Phase 1 first LNG is BP's (BP) third major project start-up in 2025, in line with its strategy of growing its upstream oil and gas business.

data.title: Micron announces market segment-based business unit reorganization
data.description:
Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) announced on Thursday a market segment-based business unit reorganization into four: Cloud Memory Business, Core Data Center Business, Mobile and Client Business, and Automotive and Embedded Business.

The stock was +1.00% Thursday pre-market to $70.02.

The innovative memory and storage solutions provider said the reorganization aims to capitalize on the transformative growth driven by AI, from data centers to edge devices.

Micron is set to begin transitioning to the new business structure immediately. The transition is expected to close early in FQ4, beginning May 30. The company will report financial results under the new business structure starting FQ4.

data.title: uniQure jumps on FDA breakthrough tag for Huntington’s disease drug
data.description:
uniQure (NASDAQ:QURE) shares added ~39% in the premarket on Thursday after the Dutch biotech announced that the FDA granted Breakthrough Therapy designation for its gene therapy candidate AMT-130.

The FDA has issued a Breakthrough Therapy tag for AMT-130 as a treatment for Huntington’s disease, a rare neurodegenerative disorder with no FDA-approved disease-modifying therapies.

The company had previously received the FDA’s Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy (RMAT) designation, Orphan Drug designation, and Fast Track designation for the one-time therapy.

The decision is backed by data from the company’s ongoing Phase 1/2 trials for AMT-130, which, according to 24-month data, have indicated a dose-dependent effect in slowing disease progression in patients with Huntington’s disease.

The FDA's Breakthrough Therapy designation aims to speed up the development and review of treatments targeted at serious or life-threatening conditions. Developers can receive regulatory guidance with potential eligibility for the FDA’s priority review for a drug with breakthrough therapy designation.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Thursday: NFLX, AXP, LLY and GPN
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures on Thursday were mixed, with market participants digesting quarterly results and economic data while keeping an eye on tariff developments. Here are some stocks to watch on Thursday: 

  • Shares of Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) will be in focus, ahead of the streaming giant's Q1 results after the closing bell. Wall Street expects the Los Gatos, Calif.-based firm to earn $5.68 per share on revenue of $10.50B. This will be the first quarter in which Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) will not disclose its subscriber count - a metric that was traditionally seen as a key performance indicator. Netflix (NFLX) shares are +7.9% YTD, significantly outperforming the broader market which has slumped amid tariff turmoil. 
  • American Express (NYSE:AXP) stock was +0.3% in pre-market trading. The credit card issuer delivered a 6% Y/Y increase in Q1 net income, as its premium base of customers continued to spend. The Dow 30 component also reaffirmed its full-year top- and bottom-line guidance, "based on the steady spend and credit trends we have seen to date and the current economic outlook." AXP's quarterly network volume came in below the Visible Alpha consensus. See how AXP performed in charts.
  • Shares of Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) soared +11.4% ahead of the opening bell. The world's largest pharmaceutical firm said its investigational GLP-1 receptor agonist orforglipron reduced weight by an average 7.9% at the highest dose in a late-stage trial. "As a convenient once-daily pill, orforglipron may provide a new option and, if approved, could be readily manufactured and launched at scale for use by people around the world," CEO David Ricks said. U.S-listed shares of rival Novo Nordisk (NVO) fell -7.6%.
  • Global Payments (NYSE:GPN) stock slipped -8% ahead of market open. The payment technology firm through separate deals said it had acquired rival Worldpay for a total value of $24.25B, including $1.55B of anticipated tax assets. GPN engineered the transaction by acquiring the 45% stake in Worldpay that FIS (FIS) still owned, and simultaneously buying the 55% stake held by private equity firm GTCR. Global Payments (NYSE:GPN) has also agreed to sell its Issuer Solutions business to FIS for $13.5B.

data.title: Utz Brands names new CFO, sets Q1 sales guidance above street view
data.description:
Utz Brands (NYSE:UTZ) has tapped Tropicana Brands CFO William Kelley as the company’s new chief financial officer, succeeding Ajay Kataria on May 1. Kelley will report directly to CEO Howard Friedman.

In other C-suite related news, Mark Schrieber, Executive VP, Sales and Chief Customer Officer will retire on May 31, succeeded by the current senior VP of Large Format, Jeremy Stuart.

The company also reaffirmed its FY25 outlook and set preliminary guidance for the first quarter.

For the quarter ending March 31, Utz Brands (NYSE:UTZ) expects sales to increase by 1.5% to 1.7% and organic net sales to be up 2.8% to 3.0%. This translates into sales of $351.7M to $352.4M versus the estimated $345.4M.

Net income is expected to range from $4M to $6M, resulting in an adjusted profit of $0.14 to $0.16 per share, versus the consensus estimate of $0.16, while adjusted EBITDA is projected to be between $44M and $46M.

For FY25, Utz Brands (NYSE:UTZ) continues to expect organic net sales growth of low-single digits led by branded salty snacks, and adjusted earnings per share growth of 10% to 15%, or $0.85 to $0.89 per share versus estimates of $0.85. Adjusted EBITDA is expected to increase by 6% to 10% and adjusted EBITDA margin expansion of ~100 basis points.

data.title: Lockheed Martin appoints Evan Scott as CFO
data.description:
• Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) announced on Thursday the appointment of Evan Scott as the company's senior vice president and chief financial officer (CFO) succeeding Jesus "Jay" Malave, who advised the company that he is pursuing other opportunities.
  • Scott, a 26-year Lockheed Martin finance executive, has served as the company's treasurer and the CFO of two business areas, said the company, adding that "the appointment is effective immediately."
  • The company will hold its first quarter 2025 earnings webcast as scheduled on Tuesday, April 22, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: DocuSign's Momentum event sees positive views from analysts
data.description:
Analysts were largely positive after attending DocuSign's (NASDAQ:DOCU) user conference Momentum held on Wednesday. 

Shares of DocuSign were largely flat premarket on Thursday.

Evercore maintained an In Line rating and $90 price target on the stock.

Evercore Analysts led by Kirk Materne said they continue to believe that  DocuSign is making solid steps towards being recognized as a 'platform' with capabilities well beyond the core e-signature, and the 10,000 IAM customer data point validates the customer appetite for a true Agreement 'platform.'

"While the macro will likely remain an overhang in the near-term, DOCU is emerging as an interesting idea heading into the 2H and into CY26, especially if IAM momentum can continue to drive strong billings/NRR [Net revenue retention] improvement," the analysts added.

Intelligent Agreement Management, or IAM, is a cloud software repository to centrally store, manage, and analyze agreements across an organization, unlocking data trapped in agreements to drive efficiencies, uncover opportunities to lower costs, and reduce risk, according to the company.

"Overall, we come away from the event feeling incrementally more positive than DocuSign’s platform story is starting to resonate in a more meaningful way with customers as DOCU continues to pioneer the analytics and insights aspect of company agreements," said Materne and his team.

Meanwhile, Needham kept its Hold rating on DocuSign.

Needham Analyst Scott Berg and his team said they attended Docusign Momentum and are impressed how quickly Docusign is pivoting to the new IAM solution, even as the existing customer base remains e-signature orientated.

IAM now has 10,000 customers. While concentrated in the small and medium-sized business, or SMB, space, this is impressive given the recency of the launch, the higher price point, and the initial focus on prerenewal expansion, the analysts added.

The analysts expect additional ramp-up as Docusign shifts SMBs to a self-service option to free up capacity for complex company-wide IAM sales.

Innovation at the conference was focused on showing the IAM platform, including a new AI agent, a centralized workflow management solution, a post-signing obligation management solution, and a significant embrace of customer experience and third-party functionality, the analysts noted.
